title: CGroups v2: crun executable file not found in $PATH
body:
Switched to CGROUPSv2, get the error message `"crun": executable file not found in $PATH` when doing `buildah run`.

This is probably a user/config issue on my end, but I dont see anywhere that I would have configured the runtime to use `crun` vs `runc`. `runc` is installed at `/usr/bin/runc`, which is in my `$PATH`. `crun` is not installed on this machine, and `runc` is my preferred container runtime (not that I know any better, just using the defaults).

My understanding is that `runc` is now capable of using CGROUPSv2. Is that not correct?

I brilliantly decided to switch my OpenSUSE Tumbleweed machine to use CGROUPSv2 (because what could go wrong lol?) and now a previously working podman/buildah instance isnt working. **This is the same for both root and non-root users.** Also, Not sure if this matters, but there is no `/etc/containers/container.conf`. I never use the root user for podman or buildah, however. 

What can I do to rectify this issue?

**Thanks in Advance for your help!**

**I get the same behavior when run as root:**
```
rocinante# whoami
root
rocinante# podman ps 
Error: default OCI runtime "crun" not found: invalid argument
rocinante# grep -nr 'crun' /etc/containers 
rocinante#
rocinante# ls /etc/containers 
mounts.conf  oci  policy.json  registries.conf	registries.conf.rpmnew	registries.d  seccomp.json  storage.conf
```


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Add kernel command line `systemd.unified_cgroup_hierarchy=1` to grub and reboot
2. Exec `buildah run tumbleweed-working-container /bin/bash `

**Describe the results you received:**
```
☢ buildah run tumbleweed-working-container /bin/bash
error running container: error creating container for [/bin/bash]: : exec: "crun": executable file not found in $PATH
error while running runtime: exit status 1
```

**Additional info:**
```
⌬ echo $PATH
/home/sbrady/.local/sbin/:/home/sbrady/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/bin
```
```
⌬ ls /usr/bin | grep runc
ntfstruncate
pamfixtrunc
runc
runcon
truncate
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

buildah to run as expected

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
☢ rpm -q buildah 
buildah-1.16.2-1.1.x86_64

```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
⌬ buildah version
Version:         1.16.2
Go Version:      go1.14.9
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  v0.7.2-0.20190904153231-83439463f784
image Version:   5.5.2
Git Commit:      unknown
Built:           Tue Sep 29 18:00:00 2020
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

```
☢ podman --version
podman version 2.1.1
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
⌬ cat /etc/os-release 
NAME="openSUSE Tumbleweed"
# VERSION="20201011"
ID="opensuse-tumbleweed"
ID_LIKE="opensuse suse"
VERSION_ID="20201011"
PRETTY_NAME="openSUSE Tumbleweed"
ANSI_COLOR="0;32"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:opensuse:tumbleweed:20201011"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.opensuse.org"
HOME_URL="https://www.opensuse.org/"
DOCUMENTATION_URL="https://en.opensuse.org/Portal:Tumbleweed"
LOGO="distributor-logo"
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
⌬ uname -r
5.8.14-1-default
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
⌬ cat ~/.config/containers/containers.conf 
# libpod.conf is the default configuration file for all tools using libpod to
# manage containers

# Default transport method for pulling and pushing for images
image_default_transport = "docker://"

# Paths to look for the conmon container manager binary.
# If the paths are empty or no valid path was found, then the `$PATH`
# environment variable will be used as the fallback.
conmon_path = [
]

# Environment variables to pass into conmon
conmon_env_vars = [
		"PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin"
]

# CGroup Manager - valid values are "systemd" and "cgroupfs"
cgroup_manager = "systemd"

# Container init binary
init_path = "/usr/bin/catatonit"

# Directory for persistent libpod files (database, etc)
# By default, this will be configured relative to where containers/storage
# stores containers
# Uncomment to change location from this default
#static_dir = "/var/lib/containers/storage/libpod"

# Directory for temporary files. Must be tmpfs (wiped after reboot)
tmp_dir = "/tmp/libpod/"

# Maximum size of log files (in bytes)
# -1 is unlimited
max_log_size = -1

# Whether to use chroot instead of pivot_root in the runtime
no_pivot_root = false

# Directory containing CNI plugin configuration files
cni_config_dir = "/etc/cni/net.d/"

# Directories where the CNI plugin binaries may be located
cni_plugin_dir = [
	       "/usr/lib/cni",
	       "/opt/cni/bin"
]

# Default CNI network for libpod.
# If multiple CNI network configs are present, libpod will use the network with
# the name given here for containers unless explicitly overridden.
# The default here is set to the name we set in the
# 87-podman-bridge.conflist included in the repository.
# Not setting this, or setting it to the empty string, will use normal CNI
# precedence rules for selecting between multiple networks.
cni_default_network = "podman"

# Default libpod namespace
# If libpod is joined to a namespace, it will see only containers and pods
# that were created in the same namespace, and will create new containers and
# pods in that namespace.
# The default namespace is "", which corresponds to no namespace. When no
# namespace is set, all containers and pods are visible.
#namespace = ""

# Default infra (pause) image name for pod infra containers
infra_image = "registry.opensuse.org/kubic/pause:3.1"

# Default command to run the infra container
infra_command = "/usr/bin/pause"

# Determines whether libpod will reserve ports on the host when they are
# forwarded to containers. When enabled, when ports are forwarded to containers,
# they are held open by conmon as long as the container is running, ensuring that
# they cannot be reused by other programs on the host. However, this can cause
# significant memory usage if a container has many ports forwarded to it.
# Disabling this can save memory.
#enable_port_reservation = true

# Default libpod support for container labeling
# label=true

# The locking mechanism to use
lock_type = "shm"

# Number of locks available for containers and pods.
# If this is changed, a lock renumber must be performed (e.g. with the
# 'podman system renumber' command).
num_locks = 2048

# Directory for libpod named volumes.
# By default, this will be configured relative to where containers/storage
# stores containers.
# Uncomment to change location from this default.
volume_path = "/home/sbrady/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes"

# Selects which logging mechanism to use for Podman events.  Valid values
# are `journald` or `file`.
# events_logger = "journald"

# Specify the keys sequence used to detach a container.
# Format is a single character [a-Z] or a comma separated sequence of
# `ctrl-<value>`, where `<value>` is one of:
# `a-z`, `@`, `^`, `[`, `\`, `]`, `^` or `_`
#
# detach_keys = "ctrl-p,ctrl-q"

# Default OCI runtime
runtime = "runc"

# List of the OCI runtimes that support --format=json.  When json is supported
# libpod will use it for reporting nicer errors.
runtime_supports_json = ["runc"]

# Paths to look for a valid OCI runtime (runc, runv, etc)
# If the paths are empty or no valid path was found, then the `$PATH`
# environment variable will be used as the fallback.
[runtimes]
runc = [ "/usr/bin/"
]

# The [runtimes] table MUST be the last thing in this file.
# (Unless another table is added)
# TOML does not provide a way to end a table other than a further table being
# defined, so every key hereafter will be part of [runtimes] and not the main
# config.
```

comments.body:
Podman has builtin support that switches to crun when you use cgroupV2. runc did not support V2 until recently, if it does at all on your platform.

Can you install crun?

Closing, expected behavior. 

Hopefully this issue is enough documentation on this for now. Thanks @rhatdan for getting back to me so quick.

@rhatdan, can you comment on what the preferred container runtime is? Looks like this team is also heavily involved in `crun`, but it doesn't appear as mature - not sure if that matters. `runc` is in the tumbleweed repos so it's easier for me, but with tumbleweed it's easy to get it added. 

Should I make a request to get it added, do you think? Should tumbleweed users be using `crun` instead of `runc` as this point? 

Thanks again, and in advance.

> Can you install crun?

No, I'm filing an issue on that.

We are trying to be OCI Runtime Agnostic.  We are currently using crun on Fedora for support for CGRoupsV2 although Red Hat has been contributing heavily to get runc up to snuff, as well as the entire Kubernetes community to handle cgroupv2.
In RHEL currently we only support runc, since we don't support crun on RHEL.  We have crun available now in tech-preview for RHEL8.3  In RHEL8.4 we will supporting Cgroup v2 with crun.

We look at crun as a place to experiment with new ideas and fixes for advanced features of Podman, and issues people are getting with rootless containers.  Then work to get these features into OCI and runc.
